author: Aristotle
topic: Monarchy as legitimate form; natural hierarchy
notes: Book III: five types of kingship; the superior king above law; monarchy as best when the ruler is supremely virtuous. Book I: natural hierarchy from household outward — the political basis of Filmer's argument

author: Plato
notes: The true statesman-king rules by knowledge not law; monarchy of the wise as the ideal

author: Plato
notes: The just city requires a philosopher-king; rule by wisdom; hierarchy in soul and city

author: Xenophon
notes: The education and character of Cyrus the Great as model king; voluntary obedience; subjects love the good king. Most influential ancient text on ideal kingship — read by Renaissance princes

author: Xenophon
notes: Dialogue on the difference between tyrant and king; the king loved by subjects

author: Cicero
notes: The ideal statesman-rector as guardian of the commonwealth; monarchy can be best

author: Cicero
notes: Natural law basis for political authority; magistrates and their oaths

author: Homer
notes: The basileus as warrior-chief; divine sanction for leadership; Agamemnon as king of kings

author: Plutarch
notes: Biographical portraits of great kings and lawgivers; monarchy in practice

author: Seneca
notes: Written for Nero; the emperor as the soul of the state; Stoic monarchy; the emperor's mercy as defining royal virtue
